factors.name: Tech selloff momentum
factors.summary: Nasdaq saw largest single-day point drop on record Friday (>1,121 points) with $1.8T S&P 500 wipeout. 4h change -1.47%, 24h change -2.73% indicates ongoing selling pressure.

factors.name: Strong US jobs data
factors.summary: Robust employment data fueling expectations Fed will keep rates higher for longer, pressuring long-duration growth multiples and AI-heavy mega-cap valuations.

factors.name: Real yields / rate expectations
factors.summary: Bond traders betting on biggest CPI surge in years this week (per Bloomberg). Higher nominal rates + inflation expectations compress tech multiples; no dovish Fed pivot priced in.

factors.name: Middle East escalation (geopolitical)
factors.summary: Iran fired missiles at Israel over weekend, fragile ceasefire threatened despite Trump negotiations attempt. Oil surged >$2; risk-off sentiment driving equity rotation away from tech.

factors.name: Sector rotation underway
factors.summary: Investors dumping tech (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT hits) and rotating into health insurers, banks, retailers per MarketWatch. Mega-cap AI/cloud names under pressure; capex concerns rising.

factors.name: Hyperscaler capex flood looming
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports wave of new mega-cap equity issuance coming to fund AI capex (AWS, Azure, GCP guidance awaited). Questions on buyer depth and dilution pressure mounting.

factors.name: NVDA-TSMC semiconductor partnership news
factors.summary: NVDA and SK hynix announce multiyear memory deal (positive for chip demand), but drowned out by broader tech selloff and rate concerns. Constructive but insufficient to counter macro headwinds.

factors.name: Japan GDP beat, yen strength risk
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP 1.8% q/q annualized vs 1.3% expected; supportive of yen. USD/JPY above 160 may trigger intervention. Yen strength = headwind for NQ (31% of Nasdaq has international revenue).

factors.name: Dollar strength from risk-off
factors.summary: AUD/USD, NZD/USD, GBP/USD all softening Monday as Middle East tensions and stronger USD drive risk-off flows. Stronger dollar pressures international earnings for mega-cap tech exporters.

factors.name: Upcoming CPI data (this week)
factors.summary: US CPI expected this week; traders positioning for inflation surprise. Any hot print = further Fed hike expectations = extended tech multiple compression risk.

key_levels.resistance: 29,200 (intraday bounce target), 29,500 (upper end of pre-Friday range)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq100 is in PHASE 2 of a sharp tech-led selloff that began Friday with the largest single-day point drop on record. The catalyst mix is potent: strong US jobs data has eliminated any near-term Fed rate-cut expectations, real yields are rising ahead of this week's likely hot CPI print, and geopolitical risk (Iran missile attacks) has triggered classic risk-off rotation out of long-duration growth names into cyclicals and defensive sectors. Mega-cap tech valuations—particularly NVDA, AAPL, MSFT—were already stretched, and hyperscaler capex concerns are mounting as a flood of new equity issuance looms. While NVDA-TSMC partnership news is constructive for chip demand, it is overwhelmed by macro headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI (likely this week Jun 10–12 range) — hot print would validate bear case, keep rate expectations high
Apple WWDC 2026 (Jun 9) — AI/cloud strategy commentary could ease or worsen sentiment
BoC interest rate decision (early June) — dovish surprise could help risk-on, hawkish keeps pressure on growth
ECB decision (early June window) — same dynamic
FOMC minutes or Fed speakers — any hint of rate hold = some relief; hike signal = tech pressure persists
Mega-cap earnings commentary on capex / cloud guidance (pending MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN updates)
Treasury 10Y yield moves — >4.30% likely = further tech compression
screening.risk_warning: Move is already -2.73% in 24h (PHASE 2). Confidence capped at 6 because: (1) Asia markets open Monday may gap down further or bounce, creating noise; (2) Trump's public statements on Iran deal (appears he still wants negotiation) could reduce geopolitical premium, spurring relief rally; (3) if CPI comes in cooler than expected or Fed signals pause, bias flips bullish quickly; (4) mega-cap oversold bounce (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT gamma / short-covering) could reverse 200-500 point move intraday.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended in 4h timeframe. Avoid aggressive shorts here. Wait for retest of 29,000–29,200 resistance (likely intraday bounce zone) before fresh short entry. If long, enter only on break below 28,700 support with tight stops; stop-loss at 28,500. CPI data this week is the key event — hold bias light until then or until technical break + Fed confirmation.
